commit.message:
fix: orgNavigate stale closure causes intermittent broken card clicks

orgNavigate reads organizationTools.organization?.name from a closure.
TaskCard's React.memo with a custom comparator doesn't check onTaskClick,
so the card retains the orgNavigate from its first render. If organization
hadn't finished loading yet (it requires 4+ sequential API calls), the
closure permanently has organization as undefined, causing router.navigate
to throw "missing parameters { org_id }".

Use a ref so orgNavigate always reads the latest organizationTools state
at call time, regardless of which render's closure it was created in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: scope Kodit MCP to project repositories (#1872)

* scope Kodit MCP to project repositories

Resolve session → project → allowed Kodit repo IDs and use
kodit.NewScopedMCPHandler to restrict MCP access per session.

- Add session_id query param to Kodit MCP URL in Zed config
- Add repo resolver (session → project → kodit repo IDs)
- Replace unscoped passthrough with per-session scoped handlers
- Cache scoped handlers per session with 5-minute TTL cleanup
- Pass store.Store to NewKoditMCPBackend / initKodit
- Upgrade kodit to v1.1.7 (adds NewScopedMCPHandler)

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

fix(ci): fix Kind networking for helm upgrade test in Drone

commit.message:
fix(ci): robust container detection for Kind networking in Drone

Drone CI overrides the container hostname, so `docker inspect $(hostname)`
fails. Switch to outcome-based detection (test kubectl connectivity) and
add fallback methods for container ID: hostname, cgroup v1 cpuset, and
mountinfo. Use grep -oE instead of -oP for BusyBox compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): fix Kind networking in Drone CI and add runner token

When running inside a Drone container with the host Docker socket mounted,
Kind's kubeconfig points to 127.0.0.1 which is the container's own localhost.
Fix by connecting the container to Kind's Docker network and patching the
kubeconfig to use the control plane container's IP. Also add the required
runner token so the controlplane pod doesn't crash on startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): address code review findings for helm upgrade test

- Add concurrency limit (1) to prevent overlapping cron runs
- Add platform constraint (linux/amd64) for Kind binary compatibility
- Add Docker-level pre-flight cleanup for orphaned Kind containers
- Add jq for reliable JSON parsing (replaces grep on JSON)
- Remove unused NAMESPACE variable
- Remove unreliable port-forward health check (helm test covers this)
- Remove fake for-loop on single service check
- Remove controlplane.providers={} override that breaks template

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): use host Docker socket for helm upgrade test

DinD (Docker-in-Docker) fails in Drone because Kind requires cgroups v2
delegation which isn't available inside a nested container. Switch to
mounting the host Docker socket, matching the pattern used by all other
Docker-dependent Drone pipelines in this repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chart: expand helm test hooks with service validation

commit.message:
fix(chart): use reliable service checks in helm test hooks

- Replace nslookup (broken in busybox/Kind DNS) with nc/wget TCP checks
- Fix HTTP status parsing (busybox wget --server-response outputs
  differently than GNU wget)
- Use nc for PostgreSQL port checks with wget fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(chart): gate typesense PVC on typesense.enabled

The typesense PVC was only gated on typesense.persistence.enabled,
not typesense.enabled. When typesense is disabled (the default),
the PVC was still created with no consumer, staying Pending forever
and causing `helm install --wait` to time out.

The deployment and service templates already check typesense.enabled
— the PVC was the only one missing this guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chart: add icon to all Helm charts

Adds the Helix logo (https://helix.ml/logo.png) to all three Chart.yaml
files, silencing the helm lint "icon is recommended" info message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: build helm dependencies before lint step

The controlplane chart depends on bitnami/postgresql and bitnami/common
subcharts. Without fetching them first, helm lint fails with
"no template common.storage.class".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chart: expand helm test hooks with comprehensive service validation

Replaces the basic wget test with a multi-step validation that checks:
- Controlplane service responds on its port
- API health endpoint doesn't return 5xx
- SearXNG, Chrome, PGVector, Kodit services are reachable (conditional)

Tests are gated on values (e.g. searxng.enabled) so they adapt to the
deployment configuration. Used by the nightly helm upgrade test and
by customers running `helm test` after upgrades.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: include pre-release versions in helm upgrade test

Use --devel flag so the upgrade test also covers pre-release chart
versions (e.g. 0.4.0-rc1), not just stable releases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add nightly Helm upgrade compatibility test

Adds a Drone cron pipeline that spins up a Kind cluster, installs the
previous published chart version, then upgrades to the latest published
version — testing the exact upgrade path customers experience.

Validates: pods ready, no CrashLoopBackOff, helm test passes, health
check responds. Slacks #helix-janitor on failure.

Requires creating `helm-upgrade-nightly` cron in Drone settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add helm lint and template validation to PR pipeline

Catches broken Helm charts before merge by linting all 3 charts
and rendering templates with all values variants (default, example,
bare-metal, EKS, GKE). Runs in parallel with existing steps (~30s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix missing specTaskCreator parameter in ExecuteCronTask call

The call to cron.ExecuteCronTask() in app_trigger_handlers.go was missing
the specTaskCreator parameter added in the CronTrigger Action/ProjectID
feature. Pass nil since manual trigger execution via the API only runs
sessions, not spec tasks.

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@3ac1a4537:001491_fix-build-failure-in

commit.message:
feat: add Kodit Queue admin page (#1861)

* feat: add Kodit Queue admin page with active task display

Add a dedicated admin page for managing the Kodit task queue, accessible
via the sidebar under "Code Intelligence". Includes paginated task list,
queue statistics (pending count, oldest task wait, operation/priority
breakdowns), priority update dialog, task deletion, and active in-progress
tasks displayed at the top with progress bars.

Also fixes:
- Repo detail navigation in admin panel (callback props instead of router)
- External repo Kodit registration (API key was only set for local repos)
- Upgrades kodit dependency to v1.1.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing KoditServicer methods to fakeKodit test double

The fakeKodit struct was missing ListAllTasks, ActiveTasks, DeleteTask,
and UpdateTaskPriority methods added to the KoditServicer interface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
test: add hash character test case for customer-reported scenario

Adds test with markdown headings (## Health Check) containing hash
characters and em-dashes, matching the pattern that triggered the
original customer bug report.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add tests for generateTaskNameFromPrompt UTF-8 truncation

- 10 table-driven tests covering: short/long prompts, multi-byte UTF-8
  (em-dashes, CJK), whitespace collapsing, edge cases (empty, whitespace-only)
- Regression test that constructs the exact byte layout from the original bug:
  em-dash at byte position 55-57 + "..." producing invalid 0xe2 0x80 0x2e
- All results validated for UTF-8 correctness and 60-rune limit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Regenerate OpenAPI spec and TypeScript client with new CronTrigger fields

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@717f5d6:001478_add-action-and-projectid

Add Action and ProjectID fields to CronTrigger

- Add Action (string: 'session' or 'spec_task') and ProjectID (string)
  fields to types.CronTrigger
- Add SpecTaskCreator interface in cron package for testability
- Branch ExecuteCronTask(): spec_task action calls CreateTaskFromPrompt,
  default/session action preserves existing RunBlockingSession behavior
- Wire SpecDrivenTaskService into trigger manager via SetSpecTaskCreator()
  setter (late-init pattern matching existing codebase conventions)
- Add 4 new tests: success, error, missing project ID, nil creator
- Update all existing test calls with new specTaskCreator parameter

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@0d27b0a:001478_add-action-and-projectid

Fix approve-specs handler setting approval fields on rejections

When approved=false (or omitted, defaulting to false), the handler was
setting SpecApprovedBy and SpecApprovedAt which is misleading, and
transitioning through spec_approved → spec_revision → spec_generation
via the orchestrator. Now rejections go directly to spec_revision and
only set approval tracking fields when actually approved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix failing unit tests: add missing mock expectations

TestChatCompletions_App_CustomProvider: The refactored findProviderWithModel
now calls ListProviders unconditionally to search cached model lists. The test
creates a fresh MockProviderManager but didn't add the ListProviders expectation.

TestFinalizeComment_CommentExists: populateAgentResponseFromSession is called
when comment.AgentResponse is empty, which calls GetSpecTaskDesignReview,
GetSpecTask, and GetSession. The test didn't expect these additional calls.

Also update CLAUDE.md to prefer Drone MCP tools over manual curl for CI.

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@3ac1a4537:001420_responses-from-the-agent
xx

show examples
route, regen
service
org api keys service
not found page
handlers
org api keys
org api keys
org sidebar

fix: truncate task name by runes not bytes to prevent UTF-8 corruption

generateTaskNameFromPrompt used byte-level slicing (name[:57]) to
truncate long task names. When prompts contain multi-byte UTF-8
characters (em-dashes, smart quotes, etc.), byte position 57 can land
mid-character, producing invalid UTF-8 that Postgres rejects with
SQLSTATE 22021 (invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8").

Switch to rune-based truncation so multi-byte characters are never split.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix flaky zed-e2e-test: ignore cleanup rm errors from stale cache

The post-build cleanup rm commands can fail when previous builds left
root-owned files on the shared Docker volume cache. Add || true so
the step proceeds to the actual docker run test instead of dying
during cleanup.

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@3ac1a4537:001420_responses-from-the-agent
fix links

Rewrite design doc to match actual implementation

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@8e9bc3d16:001420_responses-from-the-agent

Simplify /v1/models: aggregate from cache, drop unnecessary machinery

Replace 8 functions (295 lines) with inline aggregation loop that reads
from the existing provider model cache. No separate getHelixInternalModels,
getProviderModelsWithPrefix, hasConnectedRunners, etc.

When no ?provider= param: iterate cached models from all global and
DB-stored providers, prefix with provider name, skip anthropic (served
via anthropic-version header proxy).

When ?provider= specified: original single-provider behavior.

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@8e9bc3d16:001420_responses-from-the-agent

Remove model prefix mechanism, route by model list lookup instead

The anthropic/ prefix on model names was a workaround for Helix's
own provider-prefixed routing. An Anthropic endpoint only serves
Anthropic models — just forward the model name as-is.

For the outer Helix routing case, /v1/chat/completions now checks
all providers' cached model lists for unprefixed model names (not
just slash-containing ones). So claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 gets
routed to the anthropic provider automatically.

Removes: SetModelPrefix, prefixModel, modelPrefix field,
ANTHROPIC_MODEL_PREFIX config.

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@8e9bc3d16:001420_responses-from-the-agent

Address review feedback: remove URL sniffing, flatten nested fallback

- Remove isAnthropicProvider() and IsAnthropicBaseURL() URL-sniffing
  functions. Provider type detection now uses only the c.isAnthropic
  flag set at config time.

- Detect proxy mode by comparing ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL against the
  default (api.anthropic.com) rather than sniffing the URL pattern.

- Extract deeply nested agent response fallback into
  populateAgentResponseFromSession() with early returns.

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@8e9bc3d16:001420_responses-from-the-agent

Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into feature/001420-responses-from-the-agent

# Conflicts:
#	api/pkg/openai/openai_client.go

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@8e9bc3d16:001420_responses-from-the-agent

commit.message:
fix: generalize NO_PROXY bypass to all internal services, not just Chrome

Move the NO_PROXY logic from the browser package to config.ServerConfig
so it covers ALL internal service URLs: Chrome, Typesense, Tika, SearXNG,
Haystack, LlamaIndex, frontend, and Kodit. This prevents any internal
service-to-service HTTP call from being routed through a corporate proxy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: bypass HTTP proxy for internal Chrome browser service connections

When HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY env vars are set, Go's http.DefaultClient
routes all requests through the proxy, including internal requests to
the Chrome browser service (chrome:7317/chrome:9222). The go-rod library
uses http.Get() internally which goes through the proxy. The proxy
returns HTML instead of JSON, causing "invalid character '<'" errors.

Two-pronged fix:
- Add Chrome/launcher hostnames to NO_PROXY env var at startup (covers
  go-rod's internal http.Get calls in launcher.NewManaged)
- Use dedicated no-proxy HTTP client in getChromeURL() and inline the
  ResolveURL logic (eliminates redundant second /json/version request)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent stale helix-api directory from breaking Zed E2E CI (#1853)

When a previous zed-e2e-test build fails, cleanup doesn't run and
helix-api/ persists in the cached Zed source. On the next run,
`cp -r api helix-api` (where helix-api already exists) nests the
copy — creating helix-api/api/pkg/ instead of helix-api/pkg/. The
Docker COPY then places packages at /helix/api/api/pkg/ (wrong)
instead of /helix/api/pkg/, causing Go to report "does not contain
package" for all helix imports.

Fix: rm -rf stale files before copying, and add a structure check
that fails fast with a clear error message.

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

verification.payload:
tree 5fb5f9851abd65d64dade289d145fbdf7cf9da01
parent 6cc63ca55775ed41a6be38b615ad86a51881dd59
author Phil Winder <phil@winder.ai> 1773059444 +0000
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1773059444 +0000

fix: prevent stale helix-api directory from breaking Zed E2E CI (#1853)

When a previous zed-e2e-test build fails, cleanup doesn't run and
helix-api/ persists in the cached Zed source. On the next run,
`cp -r api helix-api` (where helix-api already exists) nests the
copy — creating helix-api/api/pkg/ instead of helix-api/pkg/. The
Docker COPY then places packages at /helix/api/api/pkg/ (wrong)
instead of /helix/api/pkg/, causing Go to report "does not contain
package" for all helix imports.

Fix: rm -rf stale files before copying, and add a structure check
that fails fast with a clear error message.

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

commit.message:
fix: route desktop MCP through API gateway instead of hardcoded localhost (#1850)

* fix: route desktop MCP through API gateway instead of hardcoded localhost

The desktop MCP server was configured with a hardcoded
http://localhost:9878/mcp URL, which only works when Zed runs in the
same container network as desktop-bridge. In SaaS mode (app.helix.ml),
Zed runs in a remote sandbox so localhost resolves to the wrong host.

This adds a DesktopMCPBackend that proxies MCP requests through the
Helix API gateway via RevDial to the desktop-bridge inside the sandbox
container, matching the pattern used by kodit, session, and helix-native
MCP servers.

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

* fix: mount MCP handler on desktop HTTP server for RevDial access

The desktop MCP proxy returned 404 because RevDial tunnels to port 9876
(the desktop HTTP server), not port 9878 (the standalone MCP server).
Requests to /mcp arrived at port 9876 which had no such route.

Fix: mount the MCP handler at /mcp on the desktop HTTP server (port
9876) so it's reachable through the existing RevDial tunnel. The
standalone MCP server on port 9878 is kept for backward compatibility.

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

* refactor: remove standalone MCP server on port 9878

The standalone MCP server on port 9878 never worked through RevDial.
Remove it entirely — MCP is now served exclusively at /mcp on the
desktop HTTP server (port 9876).

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

* refactor: simplify desktop MCP backend tests with httptest.Server

Replace fragile net.Pipe + manual HTTP response formatting with
httptest.Server + net.Dial, making tests more robust and readable.

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

commit.message:
fix: add no-cache headers to /config/js to prevent stale version display

The /api/v1/config/js endpoint (which serves window.HELIX_VERSION and
other deployment config) had no cache-control headers, allowing browsers
and CDNs to serve stale responses. This caused users to see old version
numbers after deployments until they hard-refreshed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(kodit): Add dynamic MCP tool documentation to agent prompts (#1839)

* Remove redundant Kodit instructions from agent prompts

Kodit MCP is already registered as a Zed context server with its own
tool descriptions. Having additional vague instructions in the user
message ("discover patterns", "find how similar features are implemented")
competes with the specific, actionable tool descriptions the LLM already
sees, potentially degrading tool usage quality.

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

* Add dynamic Kodit MCP tool documentation to agent prompts

Use kodit's MCPServer metadata (Instructions + Tools) to generate
accurate tool documentation in both planning and implementation
prompts, replacing the vague instructions removed in 5747c6cb8.

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

* Update kodit to v1.1.3

Assisted by AI. Co-Authored-By: Helix <noreply@helix.ml>

commit.message:
Add global.extraEnv to controlplane chart for proxy support

Adds global.extraEnv that propagates to all sub-deployments:
controlplane, chrome, searxng, and haystack sidecar. Enables
customers behind corporate proxies to configure HTTP_PROXY/
HTTPS_PROXY/NO_PROXY once and have it apply everywhere.

Also adds chrome.extraEnv for chrome-specific env vars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Skip subscription step in onboarding when billing is disabled

The onboarding flow now conditionally shows the subscription step based on
the billing_enabled server config:

- When STRIPE_BILLING_ENABLED=false (default): Shows 5 steps, no subscription
- When STRIPE_BILLING_ENABLED=true: Shows 6 steps with subscription required

Changes:
- Added StepType identifiers to each step for type-safe filtering
- Created visibleSteps memo that filters ALL_STEPS based on billing config
- Updated renderStepContent to use step types instead of hardcoded indexes
- Updated all step index references to use helper functions

Fixes #1460

Spec-Ref: helix-specs@4aa76413e:001460_onboarding-for-default

releases.body:
2.7.24

## What's Changed
* fix: prevent stale config/js caching after deployments by @chocobar in https://github.com/helixml/helix/pull/1851
* fix: bypass HTTP proxy for internal Chrome browser connections by @chocobar in https://github.com/helixml/helix/pull/1852
* fix: generalize NO_PROXY bypass to all internal services by @chocobar in https://github.com/helixml/helix/pull/1855


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/helixml/helix/compare/2.7.23...2.7.24

## What's Changed
* fix: prevent stale helix-api directory from breaking Zed E2E CI by @philwinder in https://github.com/helixml/helix/pull/1853


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/helixml/helix/compare/2.7.22...2.7.23

## What's Changed
* Feature/no orgs by @nessie993 in https://github.com/helixml/helix/pull/1849
* fix: route desktop MCP through API gateway instead of hardcoded localhost by @philwinder in https://github.com/helixml/helix/pull/1850


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/helixml/helix/compare/2.7.21...2.7.22

readme_html:
HelixML - AI Agents on a Private GenAI Stack

👥 Discord

Deploy AI agents in your own data center or VPC and retain complete data security & control.

HelixML is an enterprise-grade platform for building and deploying AI agents with support for RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), API calling, vision, and multi-provider LLM support. Build and deploy LLM applications by writing a simple helix.yaml configuration file.

Our intelligent GPU scheduler packs models efficiently into available GPU memory and dynamically loads and unloads models based on demand, optimizing resource utilization.

✨ Key Features

🤖 AI Agents

  • Easy-to-use Web UI for agent interaction and management
  • Session-based architecture with pause/resume capabilities
  • Multi-step reasoning with tool orchestration
  • Memory management for context-aware interactions
  • Support for multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, and local models)

🛠️ Skills and Tools

  • REST API integration with OpenAPI schema support
  • MCP (Model Context Protocol) server compatibility
  • GPTScript integration for advanced scripting
  • OAuth token management for secure third-party access
  • Custom tool development with flexible SDK

📚 Knowledge Management

  • Built-in document ingestion (PDFs, Word, text files)
  • Web scraper for automatic content extraction
  • Multiple RAG backends: Typesense, Haystack, PGVector, LlamaIndex
  • Vector embeddings with PGVector for semantic search
  • Vision RAG support for multimodal content

Main use cases:

  • Upload and analyze corporate documents
  • Add website documentation URLs to create instant customer support agents
  • Build knowledge bases from multiple sources

🔍 Tracing and Observability

Context is everything. Agents can process tens of thousands of tokens per step—Helix provides complete visibility under the hood:

Tracing features:

  • View all agent execution steps
  • Inspect requests and responses to LLM providers, third-party APIs, and MCP servers
  • Real-time token usage tracking
  • Pricing and cost analysis
  • Performance metrics and debugging

🚀 Additional Features

  • Multi-tenancy with organization, team, and role-based access control
  • Scheduled tasks and cron jobs
  • Webhook triggers for event-driven workflows
  • Evaluation framework for testing and quality assurance
  • Payment integration with Stripe support
  • Notifications via Slack, Discord, and email
  • Keycloak authentication with OAuth and OIDC support

🏗️ Architecture

HelixML uses a microservices architecture with the following components:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      Frontend (React)                    │
│                     vite + TypeScript                    │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
┌────────────────────▼────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  API / Control Plane (Go)                │
│  ┌──────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Agents     │  Knowledge   │   Auth & Sessions    │ │
│  │   Skills     │  RAG Pipeline│   Organizations      │ │
│  │   Tools      │  Vector DB   │   Usage Tracking     │ │
│  └──────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────────────┘ │
└─────────┬──────────────────────────────────┬───────────┘
          │                                  │
┌─────────▼──────────┐            ┌─────────▼──────────┐
│   PostgreSQL       │            │   GPU Runners      │
│   + PGVector       │            │   Model Scheduler  │
└────────────────────┘            └────────────────────┘
          │
┌─────────▼──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Supporting Services: Keycloak, Typesense, Haystack,   │
│  GPTScript Runner, Chrome/Rod, Tika, SearXNG           │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Three-layer agent hierarchy:

  1. Session: Manages agent lifecycle and state
  2. Agent: Coordinates skills and handles LLM interactions
  3. Skills: Group related tools for specific capabilities
  4. Tools: Individual actions (API calls, functions, scripts)

💻 Tech Stack

Backend

  • Go 1.24.0 - Main backend language
  • PostgreSQL + PGVector - Data storage and vector embeddings
  • GORM - ORM for database operations
  • Gorilla Mux - HTTP routing
  • Keycloak - Identity and access management
  • NATS - Message queue
  • Zerolog - Structured logging

Frontend

  • React 18.3.1 - UI framework
  • TypeScript - Type-safe JavaScript
  • Material-UI (MUI) - Component library
  • MobX - State management
  • Vite - Build tool
  • Monaco Editor - Code editing

AI/ML

  • OpenAI SDK - GPT models integration
  • Anthropic SDK - Claude models integration
  • LangChain Go - LLM orchestration
  • GPTScript - Scripting capabilities
  • Typesense / Haystack / LlamaIndex - RAG backends

Infrastructure

  • Docker & Docker Compose - Containerization
  • Kubernetes + Helm - Orchestration
  • Flux - GitOps operator

🚀 Quick Start

Install on Docker

Use our quickstart installer:

curl -sL -O https://get.helixml.tech/install.sh
chmod +x install.sh
sudo ./install.sh

The installer will prompt you before making changes to your system. By default, the dashboard will be available on http://localhost:8080.

For setting up a deployment with a DNS name, see ./install.sh --help or read the detailed docs. We've documented easy TLS termination for you.

Next steps:

  • Attach your own GPU runners per runners docs
  • Use any external OpenAI-compatible LLM

Install on Kubernetes

Use our Helm charts for production deployments:

  • Control Plane Helm Chart
  • Runner Helm Chart

🔧 Configuration

All server configuration is done via environment variables. You can find the complete list of configuration options in api/pkg/config/config.go.

Key environment variables:

  • OPENAI_API_KEY - OpenAI API credentials
  • ANTHROPIC_API_KEY - Anthropic API credentials
  • POSTGRES_* - Database connection settings
  • KEYCLOAK_* - Authentication settings
  • SERVER_URL - Public URL for the deployment
  • RUNNER_* - GPU runner configuration

See the configuration documentation for detailed setup instructions.

👨‍💻 Development

For local development, refer to the Helix local development guide.

Prerequisites:

  • Docker Desktop (or Docker + Docker Compose)
  • Go 1.24.0+
  • Node.js 18+
  • Make

Quick development setup:

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/helixml/helix.git
cd helix

# Start supporting services
docker-compose up -d postgres keycloak

# Run the backend
cd api
go run . serve

# Run the frontend (in a new terminal)
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev

See local-development.md for comprehensive setup instructions.
